name: Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm ( AAA )
description: This test case evaluates the ability to identify abdominal aortic aneurysm (AAA) cases using a complex multi-criteria phenotype algorithm. It tests the LLM's capability to handle three distinct case types: Type 1 (repair procedures via CPT codes), Type 2 (ruptured AAA diagnosis in vascular clinics), and Type 3 (multiple unruptured AAA diagnoses in vascular settings). The query must also incorporate age restrictions and department-specific encounter filtering, representing a sophisticated clinical phenotyping scenario that combines procedural codes, diagnostic codes, encounter types, and temporal requirements.
prompt: Find all patients aged 40-89 who have had abdominal aortic aneurysm repair procedures or have been diagnosed with ruptured abdominal aortic aneurysm in a vascular clinic, or have at least two diagnoses of unruptured abdominal aortic aneurysm from vascular encounters.

required_codes.display: Endovascular repair of infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm or dissection; using aorto-aortic tube prosthesis
Direct repair of aneurysm, pseudoaneurysm, or excision and graft insertion; for aneurysm, pseudoaneurysm, and associated occlusive disease, abdominal aorta
Abdominal aneurysm without mention of rupture
